visible_structures: basal ganglia (caudate nucleus, putamen, globus pallidus)
anatomy.hemisphere_symmetry: symmetric — The visible structures appear symmetric in this sagittal view.

tissue_findings.gray_matter: Appears normal in signal intensity and morphology.
tissue_findings.white_matter: Appears normal in signal intensity and morphology. No focal lesions are identified.
tissue_findings.ventricles: The lateral ventricles are mildly enlarged, particularly the temporal horns, which is consistent with age-related changes or mild atrophy.
tissue_findings.sulci_gyri: The sulci are mildly widened, suggesting some degree of cerebral volume loss.
tissue_findings.basal_ganglia: The caudate nucleus, putamen, and globus pallidus are visible and appear normal.
tissue_findings.thalamus: The thalamus is visible and appears normal.
tissue_findings.cerebellum: The cerebellum is visible and appears normal.
tissue_findings.brainstem: The pons and medulla are visible and appear normal.

pathology_flags.notes: Mild widening of sulci and mild enlargement of the lateral ventricles are noted, consistent with age-related changes.

global_impression: This sagittal T1-weighted image demonstrates mild cerebral atrophy, evidenced by mildly widened sulci and mild ventricular enlargement. No acute findings such as infarct, hemorrhage, or mass effect are identified.
